attacking_style: Opportunistic attacks for KOM points in breakaways or late-stage moves, typically targeting intermediate climbing objectives rather than stage wins. Shows willingness to go solo 6km+ from finish when KOM points are available.
team_tactics: Dual role creates tactical conflicts: assigned as sprint leadout domestique but autonomy to pursue personal KOM opportunities. Team appears to tolerate split priorities when sprint outcome not compromised.
terrain_preference: Intermediate categorized climbs within otherwise flat/sprint stages where KOM competition is less contested. Struggles on climbs against dedicated climbers even in breakaway scenarios.
vulnerability: Loses KOM contests to stronger climbers even with favorable positioning. Role confusion between leadout duties and personal ambitions can compromise effectiveness in both objectives.
key_patterns: Targets KOM points from breakaways despite sprint leadout duties (2 observations)
Attacks for intermediate objectives rather than stage victory (2 observations)
Loses direct climbing duels to stronger specialists despite positioning advantage (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: limited recent data with 4-year gap between observations; 2022 shows continued KOM interest but execution challenges remain
